rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO FEDERAL CREDIT UNION VS. DANIEL A. MALLEN et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 5, 2024, Line 1, PLAINTIFF SAN FRANCISCO FEDERAL CREDIT UNION'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. Hearing required. Debtor shall present proof of his student loan payments for the past three months (for example: bank statements, loan invoices). If the debtor appears by Zoom, he shall e-mail copies of the documents to the court at contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to the plaintiff too prior to the hearing. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: NIGEL CHANTER ET AL VS. GREGORY MALIN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 5, 2024, Line 2, PETITIONERS NIGEL CHANTER, LAI CHEUNG, PETER BAUMANNN, ALISON BAUMANN, TON-YUN FANG, GNOMES COVE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP, AND BABAC DOANE'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. Transferred to be heard in department 501 on December 27, 2024, at 9:30 a.m. That department handles all real property cases per SF Local Rule 8.10A1. Both parties to comply with SFLR 2.7B and deliver courtesy copies of their respective filings in support of and in opposition to the motion to department 501 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date and department no later than December 17, 2024. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF AMERICA VS. DAVIS KABAYA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 5, 2024, Line 3, PLAINTIFF BANK OF AMERICA'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. The third-party claim is denied. Third party is the spouse of defendant debtor. It is presumed that the money in the joint accounts is community property and subject to collection. Third party has not presented evidence to overcome that presumption and shown that it is separate property. (See CCP 695.020; Family Code section 910; Family Code section 760; In re Marriage of Mix (1975)14 Cal.3d 604, 611; Marriage of Harrison (1986) 179 Cal.App.3d 1216, 1226.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. If the court adopts the tentative ruling, it will execute a judicial council form of order that recites the tentative. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)
